narrative:
Sunshine prevails today and Friday along with unseasonably warm temperatures running around 10-degrees above normal for afternoon highs. Scattered afternoon showers and thundershowers return on Saturday, providing a dose of welcome rainfall, although amounts will be on the light side. Sunday will be partly to mostly sunny, mild and pleasant.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 854 photos from 211 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges met to select around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started Thursday afternoon

Warm & Excellent

Last Updated Thu April 23 at 10:34AM by Scott Stephens

Sunshine prevails today and Friday along with unseasonably warm temperatures running around 10-degrees above normal for afternoon highs. Scattered afternoon showers and thundershowers return on Saturday, providing a dose of welcome rainfall, although amounts will be on the light side. Sunday will be partly to mostly sunny, mild and pleasant.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 854 photos from 211 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges met to select around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started Thursday afternoon

Forecast Discussion

Governing high pressure situated off the Southeast Coast of the United States will promote warm and pleasant conditions through Friday.

A cold front will arrive Saturday evening with scattered afternoon showers and perhaps a thundershower. It won’t bring drought-breaking rainfall, but we will take what we can get.

Sunday looks like a nice day in the wake of the frontal passage. Pleasant weather will linger on Monday, before another warm front/cold front tandem arrives for Monday night and Tuesday. This will bring more scattered showers and thundershowers, mostly later Monday night and into Tuesday.

The upper-level wind regime over the Lower-48 next week will be characterized by fast, zonal flow. This will bring somewhat hard-to-time disturbances through the area, with another one of these scheduled to arrive sometime on Wednesday. This should promote another potential for showers during the midweek time period.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

This week's roller-coaster temperature ride continues with more dry conditions perpetuating the drought. As we look further ahead, there may be better news on sending western moisture our way for week two.
